mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly job growth (PAYEMS) signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high PAYEMS periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity that boosts corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in high INDPRO regimes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a 'stagflation' risk premium that depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in stagflation regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong consumer demand for durable goods and services, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high HOUST periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) signals weak demand conditions that reduce corporate revenues, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in low RSAFS regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.02 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in industrial production (INDPRO) and rise in unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a manufacturing slowdown and labor market deterioration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 1.5% MoM and UNRATE rises > 0.3pp MoM
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in dual-contraction regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.03 over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) combined with strong job gains (PAYEMS) signals an overheating labor market that forces monetary tightening, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.5pp and PAYEMS rises > 150k in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in overheating labor regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC > -0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.
